lead_profile_text: Moving to Vietnam on 14 July (4 weeks away). Property currently listed for sale with Good Move (~3 weeks, not selling). Mortgage £1,400/month, total bills ~£1,800/month. Traditional LTL would still run at a loss after bills. STL explored as alternative. Critical blocker: estate management company covenant may restrict STL — must be confirmed before anything progresses.
emotional_profile: Pragmatic and time-pressured — flying in 4 weeks. Frustrated property isn't selling. Open to STL as Plan B to cover bills. Not a classic STL enthusiast; primarily wants the property to pay for itself while he's in Vietnam. The contractor demand story (construction project directly opposite) resonated well.
delay_trigger_phrases: Management company already restricts things like garage modifications
Estate management company covenant may restrict STL
Touch base in a couple of weeks once I have the answer
delay_timeline: Jamie flies 14 July. Must confirm covenant clearance before proceeding. Expected to contact Zac late June / early July once he has the answer.

top_objections.text: Leasehold/covenant restriction: estate management company has strict rules; STL not yet confirmed as permitted
top_objections.zac_response: Acknowledged as a hard gate — must be confirmed before proceeding. Nothing else can progress until cleared.

top_objections.text: Figure accuracy: comparable data from nearby properties, not the estate itself — accuracy uncertain
top_objections.zac_response: Acknowledged; early mover advantage on estate if covenant clears (no direct comparable STL properties)

top_objections.text: Cash flow timing: bills come out 1st; Stayful pays 1st-5th — tight timing
top_objections.zac_response: Moving bill payment dates from 1st to ~15th of month — Jamie acknowledged this is manageable

top_objections.text: Maintenance costs and sign-off process from Vietnam
top_objections.zac_response: Maintenance pre-approved up to £300; anything above requires Slack sign-off

top_objections.text: Location is fairly remote/rural, not a typical tourist draw
top_objections.zac_response: Large construction project directly across the road is primary demand driver; Amazon and RS Components also within commuting distance

top_questions.text: Does the estate management company covenant allow short-term letting?
top_questions.answer: Unknown — Jamie must check directly with the management company. This is the critical gate before anything else can proceed.

top_questions.text: Will STL significantly outperform LTL for this property?
top_questions.answer: STL Y2 net ~£1,217/month vs LTL ~£1,217/month — essentially identical in net income terms. The real value is operational control: no sitting tenant, clean sale option when ready, fully managed from Vietnam via Slack.

top_questions.text: How does Stayful handle maintenance when I'm in Vietnam?
top_questions.answer: Pre-approved up to £300 with no need to contact you; anything above requires Slack sign-off from you.

what_moved_them: Contractor bookings as primary demand driver — large construction project directly opposite the property
Weak competition on the estate (no direct comparable STL properties = early mover advantage)
Fully managed hands-off service while in Vietnam
Maintenance pre-approval process up to £300 with Slack for anything above
what_fell_flat: STL does not dramatically outperform LTL on net income for this property — both ~£1,217/month Y2 average
Rural/non-tourist location needed active demand-driver framing to overcome

conversion_gaps.gap: Covenant clearance is a hard gate — nothing progresses until confirmed
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Jamie to contact estate management company directly and confirm STL is permitted before Zac can proceed with onboarding

conversion_gaps.gap: STL vs LTL income delta is negligible — value proposition is operational not financial
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Lead on control (no sitting tenant, clean sale option, full management from Vietnam) rather than income uplift

zac_income_intro: Annual gross £26,604, annual net to owner £14,602 (£1,217/month average). Year 1 at 80% ramp: £21,283 gross / £11,682 net. ADR ~£172/night. LTL monthly net equivalent ~£1,217/month — essentially identical to Y2 STL net.
zac_reassurance_phrases: Maintenance is pre-approved up to £300 — we don't need to bother you for anything small
You've got a construction project directly across the road — that's your demand driver, not tourists
No sitting tenant means a clean sale the moment you're ready — STL keeps that option open
